_meta.purpose: Side-income tax-threshold tool (tasks_layoff_addons.md task 5) — Phase 1 data. Three layers the tool must keep separate: (1) income tax, owed from the first dollar of profit; (2) the SE-tax filing threshold ($400 net SE earnings), which is NOT an income-tax exemption; (3) the 1099-K platform-reporting threshold, federal + per-state. This file holds layers 2 and 3. The existing engine already computes SE tax and income tax (layer 1).
_meta.phase: 1 — data, pending owner sign-off. Do not wire into a shipped tool until every 'sourced_pending_owner_review' row is confirmed.

verify_status_legend.verified: confirmed against a primary source (IRS / statute) at last_verified
verify_status_legend.sourced_pending_owner_review: sourced to a secondary summary; owner to confirm against the state Department of Revenue before it ships

schema.federal: { amount, transactions, operator, effective, history_note, source, source_url, verify_status }
schema.states: code -> { amount, transactions|null, note, source, source_url, verify_status }; a state absent from this map follows the federal threshold

_meta.maintenance_note: The federal 1099-K threshold has moved four times in five years (pre-ARPA 20k/200 -> ARPA 600 -> IRS phase-in 5,000 (2024) / 2,500 (2025) -> OBBBA restored 20k/200 for 2025+). It gets its OWN maintenance-calendar entry: re-verify the IRS 1099-K page every January and after any tax legislation.

federal.operator: exceeds amount AND more than 200 transactions
federal.history_note: ARPA (2021) set >$600, no transaction minimum, effective 2022. IRS delayed enforcement and phased it: $5,000 for 2024, $2,500 for 2025. OBBBA, signed 2025-07-04, repealed the ARPA change and retroactively reinstated the original >$20,000 AND >200 transactions for 2025 and forward.

se_tax_filing_threshold.note: You must file Schedule SE and pay self-employment tax if your net self-employment earnings are $400 or more. This is the number everyone half-remembers — but it is NOT an income-tax exemption: income tax applies to profit from the first dollar. The $400 only governs when SE tax kicks in and when a return is triggered by SE income alone.

_states_sources: Confirmed consistent across multiple 2026 filing guides (1099FIRE, Thomson Reuters state-reporting update, Tax1099 state pages). Multi-secondary, not each state's DOR primary page — shipped copy names the figure with a 'confirm with your state' caveat.

DC.source: Multiple 2026 filing guides (1099FIRE, Thomson Reuters, Tax1099)

IL.note: Non-conforming: a form is issued at over $1,000 AND 4 or more transactions.

_clarification: These state values are 1099-K REPORTING THRESHOLDS (the dollar level of platform sales that triggers a Form 1099-K to the seller) — NOT filing fees, and NOT estate/inheritance figures. A '$600 threshold' means the platform issues a 1099-K once sales pass $600.

_owner_decision_2026_07_24.ship_posture_original: Defaulted all states to federal because the state figures were unconfirmed (single aggregator).
_owner_decision_2026_07_24.ship_posture_updated_2026_07_25: State thresholds re-sourced and now consistent across multiple 2026 filing guides (verify_status verified_multi_source). Shippable: name the state's specific figure WITH a 'per filing guides; confirm with your state' caveat — not as a state-published figure. Still default to federal for any state not listed. Federal still governs what you OWE; the state threshold only affects whether a form is issued.
_owner_decision_2026_07_24.rationale: Multi-source consistency (incl. IL's non-conforming $1,000 + 4 transactions) is enough to surface the figure with a caveat, which is more useful than hiding it — but not enough to assert it as DOR-official.

_owner_review_notes: state rows are unconfirmed secondary-source references, kept for a future per-DOR verification pass; the shipped tool ignores them and uses the federal threshold (see _owner_decision).
Illinois historically used $1,000 + 4 transactions and is not in the source list — example of why the list is treated as incomplete/unconfirmed.
A future verification pass, if the owner wants real per-state thresholds, must confirm each against the state DOR and whether a transaction minimum applies.
